description: Classic Polish biszkopt relies entirely on whipped eggs for leavening. The golden ratio is 1 egg per 30g sugar per 30g flour. Potato starch (20-30% of flour weight) tenderizes crumb and reduces gluten.

per_egg.note: Classic Polish biszkopt is fat-free — fat deflates foam

technique.egg_temperature: room temperature (20-22°C) — cold eggs whip to less volume
technique.whipping_stage: whip whole eggs with sugar 8-12 minutes until ribbon stage — mixture triples in volume, pale yellow, falls in thick ribbons
technique.ribbon_test: lift whisk — mixture falls in a thick ribbon that holds shape on surface for 3 seconds
technique.flour_folding: sift flour+starch 3 times, fold in 3 additions with rubber spatula using J-motion, max 40 strokes total
technique.pouring: pour batter from low height to preserve bubbles
technique.tin_prep: line bottom with parchment only — ungreased sides let cake climb

failure_modes.fix: whip at least 10 minutes — batter must be very thick and pale
use gentle J-fold, stop as soon as no dry flour visible
do not open oven for first 25 minutes
only line bottom — ungreased sides are grip surface
batter should fill tin only 2/3 height

cooling: invert pan immediately after baking — cool upside down 30 min to prevent collapse
storage: wrap in cling film, room temperature 2 days or freeze up to 1 month

description: Polish biszkopt whips whole eggs cold-method (no heat). French genoise whips eggs over bain-marie (warm method) and adds melted butter. Genoise is denser but more moist. Biszkopt is lighter but dries faster.

biszkopt.method: whole eggs + sugar whipped cold at high speed
biszkopt.use_case: layer cakes with soaking syrup, torty

genoise.method: whole eggs + sugar whipped over 40°C bain-marie, then cooled, then fold in melted butter

mechanism: starch granules interrupt gluten strands, creating softer, more tender crumb than all-flour sponge

target.ratio: 20-30% of total flour weight replaced with potato starch

condition: when making biszkopt or any foam cake requiring tender crumb
decision: Replace 25% of plain flour with potato starch for best tender crumb
